title: CSRF Attack Detected on User Email Update API

steps.message: Proactively block the observed source IP address(es) and request patterns associated with invalid anti-CSRF tokens in the WAF, as the application may defensively terminate user sessions for too many invalid tokens, thereby stalling the attack.

Programmatically invalidate the session of the user(s) targeted by the CSRF attack on '/api/v1/user/update_email' to prevent further unauthorized actions, aligning with the KB's mention of defensively terminating sessions upon invalid token receipt.

Query the SIEM for any co-occurring or recently discovered XSS vulnerabilities, particularly stored XSS flaws within the defended functionality or reflected XSS flaws in related functions, as these can be exploited to defeat anti-CSRF defenses.

params.query: index=web_app_logs (vulnerability=XSS OR vulnerability=CSRF) host={{affected_host}} sourcetype=access_combined time_range="-24h"

steps.message: Authorize a thorough review of the application's anti-CSRF token implementation for the '/api/v1/user/update_email' endpoint. Verify if tokens are correctly tied to the user's session and if both cookie and request parameter values are robustly compared to prevent bypasses, as per KB mitigation guidance.

Upon approval, deploy a hotfix to strengthen the anti-CSRF token validation logic, ensuring proper synchronization and validation of tokens across both cookie and request parameters, and addressing any identified XSS bypass vectors, as this is a key mitigation from the KB.

Send a critical alert to the #security-incidents Slack channel and create a high-priority incident ticket in Jira for post-incident review and to track continuous monitoring of the '/api/v1/user/update_email' endpoint.

params.message: CSRF incident on /api/v1/user/update_email mitigated. Review and continuous monitoring required. Jira ticket created: {{jira_ticket_link}}
params.jira_summary: Post-incident review and monitoring for CSRF on /api/v1/user/update_email
